D8 AnthropologyL2 UnderstandReference

Pigment Trade Routes — Lapis, Cinnabar, Murex

Toggle between three antique pigment trade routes: lapis lazuli (Badakhshan → Venice), Tyrian purple (Tyre → Rome), vermilion (Almadén → Constantinople)

Ultramarine cost more than gold because lapis came only from one mine in Afghanistan, 5,000 km from Mediterranean buyers. Tyrian purple was sourced on the Phoenician coast. Cinnabar (HgS) shipped from Spain. The Silk Road and Mediterranean shipping routes were partly pigment routes.

Select a pigment to highlight its overland and sea route.
